archetype: Real-Time Subsurface Void Collapse Probability & Seismic Micro-Tremor Correlation Engine for Longwall Mining Face Advancement
description: A low-latency inference system that ingests high-frequency micro-seismic data from downhole geophones and roof bolt stress sensors to predict immediate roof collapse risks during longwall shearer operations. It correlates real-time ground vibration signatures with historical geomechanical models to trigger automated hydraulic shield retraction and personnel evacuation protocols within 500ms. The system operates in extreme dusty, high-vibration environments with strict safety-critical latency requirements where false positives halt production and false negatives cause fatalities.
rules: MUST adhere to MSHA (Mine Safety and Health Administration) Part 77.1501 safety critical latency constraints, ensuring alert generation occurs within 500ms of threshold breach.
MUST process data streams adhering to SEED (Standard for the Exchange of Earthquake Data) format while synchronizing with proprietary Shearer Positioning System (SPS) CAN bus telemetry.
MUST implement deterministic real-time scheduling (e.g., Xenomai or RTAI) to guarantee thread execution priority over non-safety critical logging tasks.
MUST incorporate 'Air Gap' validation logic where all sensor readings are cryptographically signed to prevent tampering in potentially hostile union-management dispute scenarios.
MUST utilize edge-computing models that function autonomously for 48+ hours with zero connectivity to surface servers, syncing only upon safe emergence.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use cloud-native serverless architectures for the primary inference loop, as network latency and availability in deep underground shafts (2km+) are non-deterministic and fatal to safety.
DO NOT rely on asynchronous batch processing for stress accumulation models, as the time-lag between seismic event and collapse can be seconds, not minutes.

DO NOT implement generic 'average-based' anomaly detection, as geological events are non-stationary and require time-series fractal dimension analysis to distinguish between drilling noise and structural failure.
key_technical_challenges: Synchronizing clock drift between independent sensor nodes (geophones, stress cells, laser ranging) located kilometers apart in non-GPS environments to sub-millisecond precision.
Filtering high-amplitude industrial noise from the shearer cutting head (1000+ Hz) from the subtle micro-tremors (1-100 Hz) indicative of roof strata failure without losing signal integrity.
Running complex spatial-temporal correlation algorithms on ruggedized, low-power industrial hardware with strict thermal dissipation limits in a 45°C+ environment.

recommended_stack.language: Rust — Provides memory safety guarantees without a garbage collector, ensuring deterministic real-time performance critical for safety shutdowns.
recommended_stack.framework: Actix-web (embedded mode) or custom RTOS task scheduler — Allows for fine-grained control over thread priorities and preemption.
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB (running locally on edge node) — Optimized for heavy write throughput of time-series sensor data with efficient compression for offline storage.
recommended_stack.cache: Redis (ephemeral mode) — Used only for in-flight state buffering between sensor ingestion and model inference.
recommended_stack.message_broker: ZeroMQ (REQ/REP pattern) — Chosen for its ability to operate over unreliable UDP/TCP connections with built-in reconnection logic in high-loss networks.
special_services: Fractal Dimension Calculator for Strata Stability
